back_translation: Every life must submit to the governing authorities that are above — for there is no authority except from God — and the authorities that exist have been (royally) appointed by God
Therefore, the one who opposes authority opposes what God (royally) has set up — and those who oppose will bring (royal) judgment upon themselves
For rulers are not a fear to good action, but to evil. Do you wish not to fear the authority? Do good — and you will receive praise from that authority
For he is God's servant for your good. But if you do what is evil, be afraid — for he does not bear the sword in vain — for he is God's servant, an avenger to bring (royal) wrath to the doer of evil
Therefore, it is necessary to submit — not only because of (royal) wrath, but also because of conscience
For this reason, you also pay taxes — for they are state-officials of God, devoted to this very work
Render to all what is owed — to the one due tax, tax — to the one due fee, fee — to the one due fear, fear — to the one due honor, honor
Do not owe anyone anything, except for love among one another — for the one who loves another has fulfilled the Law
For 'Do not commit adultery,' 'Do not murder,' 'Do not steal,' 'Do not covet,' and any other commandment — are summed up in this saying: 'Love your neighbor as yourself'
Love does not do evil to the neighbor — therefore love is the fulfillment of the Law
And do this — knowing the time — that the hour has come for you to wake up from sleep — for now our salvation is nearer than when we first believed
The night is far spent, and the day has drawn near. Therefore, let us cast off the deeds of darkness, and put on the armor of light
Let us walk decently as in the daytime — not in revelry and drunkenness — not in sexual immorality and indulgence in lust — not in dissension and jealousy
But put on the Lord Jesus Christ — and do not make provision for the flesh to follow its lusts
